name: Brown, Edith Morrow
major: ENGLISH LITERATURE, ANGLO-SAXON, FRENCH; B.A. with Distinction
description: To great personal charm Edith adds beauty, cleverness, enthusiasm for work and play, and a lovableness that endears her to her friends.

name: Corliss, John Johnson
description: The Liberal Arts School could not circumscribe the mathematical capabilities of "Dean," consequently, he entered the Engineering Department. If he ever "gets off" freshman math, his name will glitter from the heights of his own handiwork.

name: Lagrone, Cuthbert Michael
description: Lagrone is a man of mature experience, is well versed in the vicissitudes of married life. His many friends upon the campus predict for him a brilliant career as an educator of the youth of Mississippi.

name: Miller, Russell Hale
clubs: Y. M. C. A., 2, 3, 4, 5; Editor, "M" Book, 3, 4
"Ole Miss" Staff, 2, 3, 4, 5; Art and Feature Editor, 3, 4; Editor-in-Chief, 5
Marionettes, 3, 4, 5; Press Agent, 3; President, 4; Stage Director, 5; Associate Editor "Scream"
description: A good student, a good executive, a good friend, and an original thinker, Russell leaves Ole Miss. We expect him to do something very much worth while, for he is the kind of graduate of which any college is proud.

name: Roberts, Annie Laurie
description: She exemplifies to all who know her the things that are true and honest and just and pure and lovely. We give her a love that is real and deep.

name: Smallwood, Sarah Katherine
description: One of the dearest and bravest and truest girls Ole Miss has ever known. To every duty she has responded with a wholeheartedness that wins for her the love of all.

name: Stokes, Rebecca
description: Her unfailing cheerfulness has endeared her to all with whom she has come in contact. In the dormitories, on the campus, in the class-room, she is always happy and generous to a fault.

name: Wallace, James David
description: "Dean" Wallace, who views everything with the cold eye of science, is noted for his willingness and ability to discuss physical theories about which neither he nor anyone else knows anything.

name: Vardaman, Elizabeth
description: In her we have a girl who is sincere in scholarship, in conduct, and in friendship. Her example has meant much to the University and will always be fresh in the hearts of her friends.

name: Alexander, Laura Louise
description: As a freshman, Laura "came into her own," and her popularity has not decreased during her college career. Not only has she braved Dr. Bondurant's classical course, but has snatched many moments to keep the grass from taking "Lovers' Lane."

name: Arrington, Virginia Summers
motto: "Alas, what mortal will be so unwise, Anything dearer than himself to prize."
description: If we were casting the allegory of life, she would play the "Worldly-Wise Lady." A sparkling personality and an unusual intellect.

name: Barnes, Errol R.
motto: "Gaily the troubadour touched his guitar."
description: Few men have the diligence and persistence of Barnes, and if this same diligence is continued in his profession, his reward is certain. We wish for him the best.

name: Barry, Kathryn Lee
description: Katie Lee left us at the end of her Freshman year and wandered in other "climes" for a while. She's back with us again, however, and with her dry wit and friendliness, has made friends anew.

name: Bass, Mary Lou
description: Woman's College gave us a good student and loyal friend in Mary Lou. She is jolly and likable, and those who can claim her friendship are fortunate.

name: Beasley, Willis Carl
description: This jovial preacher is known far and wide over North Mississippi as a fine sport and an enthusiastic minister. Phi Sigma will lose much of its grandeur when his booming voice is quieted within its halls.

name: Brown, Ruth
description: Ruth is a friend anyone would be glad to have. Those who know her best are most sure of her worth, merit and ability.

name: Clements, Roley Erastus
description: Quiet and unassuming, this man has accumulated a host of friends on the campus, and during his stay with us has proved himself a worthy son of 'Bama as well as a valuable asset to Mississippi.

name: Comer, Leon L.
description: "Shorty" Comer holds the world's record for efficiently managing a basketball team. He's managed a lot of friends, too.

name: Cowart, Beulah Gladys
description: Gladys is an ever-present proof that one can live happy and yet never be noisy, for she is soft of voice, sweet of disposition, modest and brilliant in scholastic work.

name: Cox, Hubert Elliot
description: Hubert's pleasing personality and jolly good fellowship have made him a host of friends who regret to see him depart from his Alma Mater.

name: Crawford, John Ama
motto: "Another fond lover, in faith."
description: A man of sterling qualities and exceptional abilities, coupled with the determination to stand by his convictions. He has a record of attendance at the coop, which would, if transferred to the class room, be a delight to any of his professors.

name: Denton, Nola
description: Nola is gifted with many talents, but especially will we remember her for her star basketball-playing during her four years with us. However, she has the ability to put over whatever she undertakes.

name: Dodds, Urlyne
description: Urlyne's full of fun and attractive as can be. She's one of those at Ole Miss who can count every one her friend.

name: Dunlap, Willie Webb
description: Willie Webb is one of our athletic heroines and general favorites. In life as on the basketball court she may be downed but never out.

name: Googe, Paul Rogers
description: Paul is a gifted youngster whose genius is dimmed only by an abnormal predilection for slumber. However, few students have any more friends or have made a better scholastic record.

name: Hastings, Velma Lane
description: Velma has that enthusiasm which is proof against all disappointments, a sort of indomitable optimism. Her personality is further enriched by her vivacious interest in French.

name: Hathorn, Percy
description: A good student, an athlete of no mean ability, and a jolly good fellow—that is Percy.

name: Healy, George William, Jr.
motto: "Leave it to George!"—an all-round man of merit in the fields of Journalism, Romance and Politics, often combining and reconciling the three with remarkable success. The scope of his activities is the most convincing evidence of his ability, and his honors the best gauge of his popularity.
clubs: Advance Manager, Ole Miss Musical Club, 3

name: Henderson, Kathryn
description: Entrancing colors—graceful lines—a bit of fashion—a pleasing ensemble, Kathryn came to us from M. S. C. W. She is the sort of girl who reminds us of dollars, champagne and limousines.

name: Hodge, Alfred Hume
description: Alfred is a conscientious student, possessed of ability without pretentiousness, and with talents in the same direction of his great namesake. We prophesy an equal success for him in the field of Education.

name: Hodges, Filo Augustus
description: He is short in stature but long in reason and likable qualities. Graduating in three years is only one of the scholastic feats which he has accomplished.

name: Holland, James Leland
description: Debating, formal or informal—oratory or "bull"—that's Leland's best bet. Having spent three years at Millsaps, he was promoted to Ole Miss, where he soon became a leader of his class.

name: Hughston, Sanford R.
description: Sanford may be justly termed the Bolshevik king of the graduating class. His unusual intellectual power, liberal education and keen perception make him one of the leading thinkers in the new school of collegiate thought. This famed toreador will be missed in the nightly meetings of the radical club.

name: Phelan, H. Hunter, Jr.
motto: "Let me live in a house by the side of the road, And be a friend to man."
description: Neither Beau Brummel nor a Rah! Rah! Boy—Phelan is the type of man whom we consider really collegiate. A sincere student and a popular one, never known to hurry, always dependable.

name: Helena, Corvette Ingram
description: Just a slip of a girl with a bit of deviltry in the sparkle of her eye. Although she has been with us only one year, we have found in her the essentials of a good comrade.

name: Emma, Grace Johnson
description: "Jonnie" couldn't stay away from the "ole home" town any longer, so after two years spent among "Mississippi's Sweetest Collection of Women," she returned to resume her work among these "Wooded Tempered Hills." Variety is the spice of life and Emma Grace can tell you that this is the place to find a "New Man."

name: Sara, Junkin
description: "What a striking looking girl!" newcomers say of Sara. Sincerity is the keynote of her personality. When Sara graduates, Ole Miss bids farewell to one of its most popular and talented students.

name: Mathilde, Killingsworth
description: Because she possesses more than the ordinary human's share of that quick sympathy, that listens to all, and helps in the very listening—do we predict that she will be a success in whatever she undertakes.

name: Landrum, Ralph Llewellyn
description: Persistence is Ralph's biggest help in putting his ability to work. Remaining out of school two years, he returned to lead the way to graduation and to Ward Hall.

name: Lawton, Susie Mae
motto: "Work is Work, and must be done, Yet as I work I have my fun."
description: We wonder if it is the natural sweetness of Susie Mae that is responsible for her being a member of every Refreshment Committee on the campus.

name: Lenoir, Doris Lamkin
description: Doris has won the admiration and respect of the student body, for she not only studies and enjoys her work, but always has time to lend a helping hand in any of the college activities.

name: McCharen, William Knox
description: Knox is frank and unassuming. He is a valuable track man, and the fact that he is a good student is amply demonstrated by the fact that he is finishing in three years.

name: Myres, Smith Macdougal
clubs: Vice-President, First Speaker, Secretary, Reporter and Doorkeeper of Phi Sigma
News Editor, Departmental Editor, Reporter, Editor-in-Chief of the 'Mississippian'
Editor, Y. M. C. A. Handbook
description: A fiery Scotchman, worthy representative of the 'Clan of MacDougal,' Mac has made one of the best editors the Mississippian has ever had.

name: Mcguire, Virginia Fae
motto: "Give every man your ear, but few your voice."
description: This would be a dull world, were it not for people like Virginia, who seem to get fun out of everything.

name: Mclean, George Alonzo, Jr.
description: George is justly famed for his integrity and his conscientious adherence to the path of duty. His many friends wish him success in his chosen field of Christian service.

name: Oberschmidt, Mary Alford
description: Mary has shown ability in many 'lines' and mastery of quite a few, including art, music and sophomore English.

name: Oldham, Dorothy Zollicoffer
description: One of the most accomplished and attractive girls Ole Miss has ever known. Her forceful personality will make her very successful in the legal profession which she has chosen.

name: Oliphant, Jefferson Davis
description: J. D. has affiliated himself with the best forces at Ole Miss. We will feel a distinct loss in his going, and feel that his place will be difficult to fill.

name: Perkins, Alton Parker
description: It is rumored that "Perk" has left more broken hearts in the Delta than he has bruises on opposing linesmen. If that be true he can have our "Loving Cup." He has been prominent in many campus activities, and is known everywhere as a hard fighter, an excellent student and a dependable man.

name: Pickens, Clara Loyce
description: One can rely on Loyce to do the correct thing at the correct time, and to do her bit toward making the world a little merrier and happier.

name: Purser, Jennie Meaders
description: Shy, modest and yet somewhat coquettish—that is Jennie Meaders. In a certain way, she has been interested in literature, law and medicine. Her smile will be missed when she leaves us.

name: Salmon, Mary Pickens
description: Though there is not so much of her, what there is is just what one likes to find. Her disposition is unsurpassed, for her good humor is the first thing one knows about Mary.

name: Smith, Sydney Louise
motto: "And still the wonder grew, that one small head should carry all she knew,"
description: Is really true in the case of Louise.

name: Stevens, Sara
motto: "Laugh and the world laughs with you,"
description: According to the doctrine of Sara. Nevertheless, she isn't afraid of work, but pursues her studies with an indomitable will.

name: Stewart, Martha Jane
description: She is clever, original, talented, smart without being especially studious. Martha Jane is of the type we would like to copy, but she is inimitable with a manner all her own.

name: Strong, Blanton Hill
description: The Mighty "B," famous for ability with visiting ladies in making them fall in love. He has the marks of popularity without the characteristics of a politician.

name: Teat, Loraine
clubs: Undergraduate Representative, Y. W. C. A.
Y. W. C. A. Representative to Blue Ridge
Social Chairman, Y. W. C. A.
description: It was a loss to the Capital City, but a gain in many ways for Ole Miss when Loraine entered the University. Charm of manner and of face have made her a campus figure.

name: Tomlinson, George Edmund
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Promotion Committee
description: "Dainty-Foot" has shown himself capable in many ways, for not only has he won a B.A. degree in three years, but has also taken an active part in other school activities.

name: Williams, Mary Louise
description: Brimming over with the joy of living and with good will, her brown eyes constantly twinkling with fun, she has made a place for herself at Ole Miss that will be hard to fill when she has gone.

name: Windham, John Evans
description: Johnny tried football as a reserve for three years, coming through with a bang his last year. He's a regular end now, but his chances for future greatness are without end.

name: Wood, George Hardin
description: "Chesty" came to us from Batesville, bringing his baseball ability with him. But he has been as popular upon the campus and in the classroom as upon the diamond.

name: Yowell, Monna
description: Look for a quiet, unassuming girl, sitting in a corner, absorbed in a book, and you've found Mona. Not that she studies all the time, for she has found time to corner her share of friendships as she went along.

name: Bradford, Thomas R.
major: Bachelor of Science in Commerce, School of Commerce and Business Administration
description: Nothing flashy about this fellow, but in making up a financial statement of the School of Commerce and Business Administration, we would list him as an asset.

name: Butler, George Washington
description: George is a noted ladies' man on the campus, but he has made a name for himself in the commercial as well as in the social world, having been for several years a capable instructor in the Business Department.

name: Conger, James Frank
description: "Red," as he is called by his friends, is quite an authority on accounting. The Mississippi Delta needs the aid of men like him, for his word is as good as gold.

name: Gaddis, Wesley Taylor
description: Wesley is a product of Soule down in New Orleans. He liked the idea of a commercial education at Ole Miss, so he decided to make a change. In Wesley we find a man capable of advancing theories on accounting methods.

name: Lott, James Ridgeway
description: Jimmie did not like the idea of getting a B.S.C. from A. & M., so he took up his "lot" with us. Probably he will like bookkeeping in Indianola. Right now he likes the walk that leads to the "coop," and especially Ward Hall.

name: Baxter, William N.
clubs: Ole Miss A. S. C. E., 3, 4, 5, 6
description: If Newt is as persistent in after life as he has proved to be in all phases of his college career, we feel assured a successful future awaits him.

name: Bruce, Robert Nolan
clubs: Ole Miss A. S. C. E., 2, 3, 4
Student Assistant in Mathematics and Mechanical Drawing, 4
description: Whenever there is anything to be done right Nolan is the man to be called on. Industry and patience deserve the reward of success.

name: Buford, Paul C.
description: "Brainey" hails from the hills of Tennessee and although he has been with us only three years, he has made a lasting impression among his many friends.

name: Mauney, James Pinkney
clubs: Treasurer, Ole Miss A. S. C. E., 4
description: J. P., "Squire," is a diligent sort of chap who hears much and says little. In his quiet unassuming way he has won the friendship of many.

name: West, Louie Lane, Jr.
description: We are undecided as to whether to call Louie the "sheik" or the "Beau Brummel" of the Engineers. Certainly his unusual gift of lung power has been turned to advantage in leading the cheering sections.

name: Barbour, Jeptia Sterling
clubs: Bachelor of Arts, University of Mississippi, 4
description: "Jep" got off to a poor start by entering Millsaps, but he finished right at "Ole Miss." He is a good student who plays at "sheiking."

name: Barnett, Ross R.
description: This willowy orator left the wilds of South Central Mississippi seeking pastures new. He is possessed of a liquid eloquence precipitated from his smiling lips in cadence with the elastic motions of his hands.

name: Berry, Ovie L.
motto: "When I don't know whether to fight or not, I always fight,"
description: Berry has won a host of admirers from his aggressive devotion to Ole Miss.

name: Brown, Joseph Eckford
motto: "A friend whose zeal outruns his promise."
description: Having survived murder by Kincannon, Joseph has lived on to manage Ole Miss '26. No political rally is complete without him nor a bull session minus one of his tales.

name: Buchanan, Frank J.
description: Frank ended his wandering career at Ole Miss. Here he has been successful in obtaining, besides a wife and family, degrees in both Lit and Law. They do no wrong who call him Judge, themselves they do merit who call him friend.

name: Campbell, Charles S.
motto: "Sigh no more, ladies, sigh no more; men were deceivers ever."
description: The delight of the ladies, the pride of the Delta and the hope of the Law School, "Sugar" is a type of man whose place will be hard to fill when he has left us.

name: Cassedy, James W., Jr.
description: Jim came to Ole Miss from the Naval Academy, imbued with all its lofty traditions of courtesy, but without a trace of its formal rigidity. During his brief residence on the campus he has won a wife and a host of friends.

name: Cunningham, Thomas Cullen
motto: "Red"
description: "Red" is a gifted orator, a shrewd business man, and an excellent student. He did his bit for America in the World War and has since that time done more than his bit for Ole Miss.

name: Davis, John Elwynne
motto: "He who loves not wine, women or song, Remains a fool his whole life long."
clubs: J. P., Ole Miss Practice Court
description: Smiles, freckles and a good "line" make Johnnie a prince of good fellows as well as the ladies' choice.

name: Floyd, James C.
major: Bachelor of Laws, B.S., A & M College, '24
description: Jim learned his multiplication tables at A. & M. He is the trump card of the law school, and has "tromboned" his way into the heart of every Ole Miss student.

name: Stanton, A. Hall
motto: "Ah, take the cash in hand and waive the rest."
description: Here is a lawyer who is the demon of Mississippi financiers, judging by the success of the various Ole Miss organizations that have been managed by him.

name: Elmer, E. Holley
description: We would not expect so much "savoir faire" from this product of Itawamba County, but he has proved a capable student and a dependable member of the legal brotherhood.

name: Holden, V. Kincannon
motto: "'Tis not the robe that makes the monk," nor does size measure the man.
description: "Skeet" proved himself a dynamo of efficiency in the local post office as well as in the Senior law courts.

name: Hubert, S. Lipscomb
description: A celebrity hindered only by his own versatility. Journalist, lawyer, orator or politician, this man may some day be.

name: Otho, Magee
description: Otho probably knows more men on the campus than any other student. His argumentative force and ingratiating manner are earmarks of legal success.

name: Montjoy, Paul Dyche Jr.
motto: "Staunch of heart and fleet of foot."
description: As "little Monty," Paul came to us one of the freshest Freshmen of five years ago. Daunted by nothing he proves that to live enough is to conquer.

name: Roberts, Malcolm Mattie
motto: "Mine epitaph shall be my name alone."
description: Ole Miss lawyers will not soon forget Roberts. Large was his bounty and his soul sincere.

name: Schierbaum, George William
description: George is one of those likeable chaps who has that rare faculty of making friends with everyone. We predict that he will be one of the foremost barristers of the state.

name: Smith, Lemuel Augustus Jr.
description: If the ladies will give him enough spare time a great future lies in store for him. He is always a magnet for damsels fair.

name: Stribling, Ethelbert Dees
major: BACHELOR OF LAWS, B.A., University of Mississippi, 4
description: Cool-headed and shrewd, a wizard with men and women, Dees has been the mastermind in campus politics for a year or so. He now is prepared to transfer his field of endeavor to the entire Magnolia State.

name: Thompson, Robert W. Jr.
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Board of Directors
description: "Robust Robert," the ladies' choice, is our selection for All-American "heart smasher," "bull shooter," "he-man" champion of his own convictions.

name: Wells, William Steven Jr.
description: Our Beau Brummel from Jackson formerly held world's record as ladies' man at the Coop. Now, though, he's strictly a lady's man.

name: Wilson, Charles D.
description: "Slim" is the sort of fellow who makes a good lawyer. Possessor of a steady nerve and presence of mind to guide him through the pinches.

name: Cotten, W. F.
description: Here we have a who has put aside his blankets and war paints to don the raiments of a professional man. In him we find the keynote to success—namely, interest in his work.

name: Davis, Donovan Forrest
description: In we have a rare combination—a true friend, a good student and a strong character. Unless caught in the net of matrimony by some of his trained nurses, he will ascend high in his profession.

name: Dickens, Robert Dubois
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Promotion Committee, 1
description: Studious, energetic and friendly to everybody—with such qualities as these, cannot fail to succeed in his chosen profession. The future holds much in store for him.

name: Douglas, Featherston
description: is the kind of a fellow who will always be a glory to the ranks of medicine. He is quiet and unassuming, but can always be counted upon to do his part in any task.

name: Eubanks, Oscar Gray
description: as he is popularly known on the campus, is a leader in his classes, and at the same time an athlete of no mean ability. No doubt, in the future, the name of Dr. Eubanks will be a synonym for immediate relief to suffering humanity.

name: Godman, George Everard
description: Not quite a George Washington, but one of the best fellows in the Medical School. Godman is a good student and will succeed as a doctor.

name: Hagamian, Adrian Van Dyke
description: The song bird of the campus is graceful in all roles. Whether it be serenading, studying or spoofing, "Dyke" can qualify.

name: Hand, George Francis
description: With a carefree abandon George has always been there with "the goods." 'Twas never said he'd not do his part, and surely he cannot fail in the study of medicine.

name: Holmes, Edward Warren
description: Pride of Winona! "Shotgun's" weakness for Titian blondes has made him an authority on femininity, for he "learned about women from them."

name: Hinton, Hebie
description: Hebie will be missed next year. He is one of the most popular men in the medical school and a hard worker. We prophesy a great career for him.

name: Johnson, Robert Harvey
description: From his quiet manner one would not suspect that this rangy lad has been a terror to opposing cages for four seasons. A brilliant athlete, a good student and a fine fellow, the Class of '26 is proud to claim him as her own.

name: Little, Joe Hollis
description: "Red" is equipped with a brilliant mind and eagerness to work, a good disposition and a pleasing personality.

name: Magee, Louis McNair
description: Maggie's transfer from A. & M. shows in itself his ability to choose the right—even though he had once erred from the path of learning by mingling with the cowpunchers. He is a hard worker and is assured of all success in his chosen profession.

name: Magruder, Levin Freeland
description: Another former Aggie who has made good at Ole Miss in his profession with his talent for art and his attractive personality.

name: McLean, Robert Beatty
description: Bob is the type of fellow that we must know in order to appreciate fully. He possesses an especially admirable quality—the will power to stick to what he considers the right, regardless of public opinion.

name: Daniel, George Melvin
description: "Hi" has the distinction of being the tallest man that has ever traveled the campus at Ole Miss. He is known from the greenest Freshman to the most intelligent Senior. With his personality and intelligence he is certain to become a great doctor.

name: Tempel, Montie Moore
description: Whether Tempel works or plays he does it with whole-hearted vim. He was a timid boy during his first four or five years with us; but now, during his Senior year he is considered the "Sheik" of the class.

name: Clifton, Tate Morris
description: Morris has made the Medical Club a good president and he will make some city a wonderful surgeon. We will miss him next year and it will be difficult to fill his place.

name: William, Walton Nobles
motto: "To err is human,"
description: so Bill redeemed himself by leaving A. & M. to study medicine at Ole Miss. If jovial manner, ambition and personality count, Bill is surely on his road to success.

name: Joseph, John Noto
description: Noto is one of the most popular boys in the medical school. He likes his work and great things are expected from him.

name: Purser, Thomas Jr.
description: Everyone speaks well of Tommy. If he only succeeds in life as he has at Ole Miss, the Medical World will surely hear of "Doc" Purser.

name: Ramer, Edwin Clyde
description: Girls, you haven't a chance with this "Sheik." He is already happily married and working hard to make a success for the lucky lady. We'll miss him next year.

name: Sevier, Sherrill Jefferson
description: Besides being the most likable man in the Medical School, "Brisco" is one of the steadiest. He didn't do much at L. S. U., but he certainly made use of his time here.

name: Stuart, Frank Allen Jr.
description: In Stuart, Millsaps' loss has been our gain. He is a prince of a fellow and a good student. Who knows, but that in years to come, Mayo Brothers may welcome this young surgeon.

name: Turner, Wilson Hudson
description: Hudson did not have far to come from the neighboring town of Water Valley to the Ole Miss campus, and he has made an exceptionally good record while with us. After two more years in Medical School he'll be ready for the world and we're sure that it will extend to him a hearty welcome.

name: White, Alvyn W.
motto: "God heals, the doctor takes the fee."
description: "Venus" has helped to maintain the Ole Miss reputation of having representatives from all the states, for Alvyn came to Oxford from Pensacola with the intention of settling down to work.

name: Wilkins, William Thomas
motto: "What's worth doing is worth doing well,"
description: Billie's motto has evidently been, "What's worth doing is worth doing well," for he has certainly done well while here. His congenial nature has carried him far on the road of friendship and although he has spent many a serious moment in the classroom and on the campus, his merry laugh will be remembered long after he has left these halls.

name: Zeltzer, Meyer
clubs: G. U. Specialist, George Hall Clinic
description: "Bromo" hails from the Kodak City; who knows but that he himself will be fitting lenses. With his friendly disposition and strong determination, he is certain to succeed in any specialty he chooses.

name: Hamill, William Erwin
description: Hamill, exponent of the perpetual grin, possesses more than a pleasing personality. He's one of the best students Doc Faser has had in his department, and has the additional honor of being graduated with the famous "Hungry" Guy.

name: Luter, J. Sisco
description: Likable! That's Luter all over. Pill rolling has come naturally with this lad, and he should soon be a master in the profession.
